settings.title: Product Catalog for Northpeak Outdoors
settings.summary: We shot a 60-piece gear catalog in studio with consistent lighting and quick turnaround.
settings.description:
Northpeak needed clean, consistent product images for 60 items ahead of their fall catalog and online store update. The gear ranged from small hardware to bulky packs, and it all had to look like it belonged in one catalog and one store grid.

The brief

Two pressures shaped the job. The look had to be uniform across all 60 pieces, so the catalog and the product grid read cleanly, and everything had to be shot and delivered in time for a fixed fall launch with no room to slip.

How we shot it

We set up a controlled studio rig and kept it fixed for the whole run, so every piece was lit and framed the same way regardless of its size or finish. We worked through the 60 items on a set schedule, then handled retouching and packshots in the same controlled pipeline to keep the output uniform.

  • A fixed studio lighting rig for the full run
  • Consistent framing across all 60 products
  • Retouching and clean packshots for the store
  • Delivery against a fixed catalog schedule

The challenge was holding that consistency across such different products. A small metal clasp and a large pack do not light the same way by default, so we built the rig to handle the range without changing the setup, which is what kept the whole catalog looking like one shoot.

The result

All 60 products were shot and delivered in under two weeks, and the catalog and store launched on time with a uniform look. Because the rig stayed fixed, the product grid lines up cleanly and nothing looks like it wandered in from a different shoot.
